member.name: M:NPBehave.Clock.AddTimer(System.Single,System.Int32,System.Action)
member.param: number of times to repeat, set to -1 to repeat until unregistered.

member.name: M:NPBehave.Clock.AddTimer(System.Single,System.Single,System.Int32,System.Action)
member.summary: Register a timer function with random variance
member.param: deviate from time on a random basis
number of times to repeat, set to -1 to repeat until unregistered.

member.name: M:NPBehave.Clock.AddUpdateObserver(System.Action)
member.summary: Register a function that is called every frame

member.name: M:NPBehave.Cooldown.#ctor(System.Single,System.Single,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,System.Boolean,NPBehave.Node)
member.summary:
The Cooldown decorator ensures that the branch can not be started twice within the given cooldown time.
The decorator can start the cooldown timer right away or wait until the child stopps, you can control this behavior with the `startAfterDecoratee` parameter.
The default behavior in case the cooldown timer is active and this node is started again is, that the decorator waits until the cooldown is reached and then executes the underlying node. You can change this behavior with the `failOnCooldown` parameter to make the decorator immediately fail instead.
member.param: random variation added to the cooldown time
If set to true the cooldown timer is started from the point after the decoratee has been started, else it will be started right away.
If set to true the timer will be reset in case the underlying node fails.
If currently on cooldown and this parameter is set to true, the decorator will immmediately fail instead of waiting for the cooldown.

member.name: M:NPBehave.Repeater.#ctor(System.Int32,NPBehave.Node)
member.param: number of times to execute the decoratee. Set to -1 to repeat forever, be careful with endless loops!

member.name: M:NPBehave.Node.Stop
member.summary: TODO: Rename to "Cancel" in next API-Incompatible version

members.member: THIS ABSOLUTLY HAS TO BE THE LAST CALL IN YOUR FUNCTION, NEVER MODIFY ANY STATE AFTER CALLING Stopped !!!!
THIS IS CALLED WHILE YOU ARE INACTIVE, IT's MEANT FOR DECORATORS TO REMOVE ANY PENDING OBSERVERS

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.None
member.summary: The decorator will only check it's condition once it is started and will never stop any running nodes.

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.Self
member.summary: The decorator will check it's condition once it is started and if it is met, it will observe the blackboard for changes. Once the condition is no longer met, it will stop itself allowing the parent composite to proceed with it's next node.

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.LowerPriority
member.summary: The decorator will check it's condition once it is started and if it's not met, it will observe the blackboard for changes. Once the condition is met, it will stop the lower priority node allowing the parent composite to proceed with it's next node

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.Both
member.summary: The decorator will stop both: self and lower priority nodes.

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.ImmediateRestart
member.summary: The decorator will check it's condition once it is started and if it's not met, it will observe the blackboard for changes. Once the condition is met, it will stop the lower priority node and order the parent composite to restart the Decorator immediately.

member.name: F:NPBehave.Stops.LowerPriorityImmediateRestart
member.summary: The decorator will check it's condition once it is started and if it's not met, it will observe the blackboard for changes. Once the condition is met, it will stop the lower priority node and order the parent composite to restart the Decorator immediately. As in BOTH it will also stop itself as soon as the condition is no longer met.

members.member: CAUTION: EXPERIMENTAL !!!! target to move blackboard key containing either a Transform or a Vector. acceptable tolerance should stop when in tolerance frequency to check for changes of reaching the destination or a Transform's location random variance for updateFrequency
